.ddx/executions/20260504T224253-6eac9246/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260504T224253-6eac9246/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260504T224253-6eac9246/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260504T224253-6eac9246/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260504T224253-6eac9246/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260504T224253-6eac9246/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-aede917d/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"# no_changes_rationale — ddx-aede917d

## Outcome
no_changes — blocked on upstream Fizeau release.

## Why this attempt produced no commit

This bead is explicitly marked `blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6` in
its labels and states in its description:

  > This bead is downstream of Fizeau agent-63bf58a6 — depends on its
  > tagged release. Until that lands, DDx falls back to today's
  > behavior (failing beads on quota errors).

Implementing AC1–AC9 requires a typed `NoViableProviderForNow` error
(with `retry_after` and `exhausted_providers` fields) returned from
the Fizeau dispatcher. The drain loop must be able to type-assert this
error to distinguish quota pause from `execution_failed`.

## Verification that the upstream is not yet released

1. cli/go.mod pins `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.0`.
2. `grep -rn NoViableProviderForNow` in
   `~/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.0/`
   returns no Go-source matches. The only mention is inside the
   embedded `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, where Fizeau bead `agent-63bf58a6` is
   still `status:"open"`.
3. No tagged Fizeau release containing the new error type is available
   to upgrade to.

## Why we cannot proceed via a `replace` directive

User-pinned policy (memory: feedback_no_local_replace) — DDx must
always import properly tagged agent releases; no `go.mod replace`,
even temporarily. Implementing this bead against an in-tree or
file-replace Fizeau would violate that rule and produce a commit that
cannot be merged.

## What a follow-up needs

1. Fizeau ships a tagged release (≥ v0.11.0 or a dedicated tag) that
   exports `NoViableProviderForNow` with `RetryAfter time.Time` and
   `ExhaustedProviders []string`.
2. `cli/go.mod` is bumped to that release.
3. Then implement, in the drain loop (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
   and cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*.go):
   - Type-assert the dispatcher error; on `*NoViableProviderForNow`,
     do NOT mark `execution_failed`. Release the claim (worker-state
     transition only — bead status unchanged per
     TD-031 §8.3 QuotaPauseContract).
   - Append `unclaimed` to the bead's event stream and append
     `drain-paused-quota` to the worker-scoped event log with
     canonical name from TD-031 §8.3.
   - Sleep until `min(retry_after, now+--quota-pause-max)`; re-probe
     by attempting the next ready bead. Backoff if still
     `NoViableProviderForNow`.
   - If `retry_after > now + --quota-pause-max`, exit with operator
     message: `"quota exhausted, expected recovery <ts>; relaunch
     when ready"`.
   - Add `--quota-pause-max` flag (default 30m) to `ddx agent
     execute-loop` and `ddx work`.
4. Surface `drain-paused-quota` worker events in
   `cli/internal/server/workers.go` so the GraphQL workers list shows
   paused workers.
5. New test (cli/cmd or cli/internal/agent): spawn 2 workers, simulate
   one provider quota-exhausting, assert claim released and sibling
   picks the bead up.
6. Confirm `schema_compat_test.go` round-trip still passes (no schema
   change is needed — TD-031 §8.3 is explicit that this is a
   worker-state concern only).

## What is done

Nothing committed. The bead remains executable as soon as the
upstream tag is published; this rationale documents the block so the
post-merge reviewer does not flag it as `no_evidence_produced`.

.ddx/executions/20260504T222154-51a1e706/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260504T222154-51a1e706/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260504T222154-51a1e706/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260504T222154-51a1e706/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260504T222154-51a1e706/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260504T222154-51a1e706/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

rationale: Bead ddx-af187382 is already satisfied at base-rev 2b34bfd0.

What is done
------------
The five AC items asserting the absence of `RunOptions`, `CompareOptions`,
and `QuorumOptions` from cli/internal/agent/grade.go,
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go, and cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go
all hold today:

  $ grep -nE 'RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions' \
      cli/internal/agent/grade.go \
      cli/internal/agent/service_run.go \
      cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go

  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:34:  // CompareRuntime is the SD-024 successor to CompareOptions for the
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:41:  // See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 CompareOptions
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:358: // QuorumRuntime is the SD-024 successor to QuorumOptions for the
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:365: // See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 QuorumOptions

The remaining matches in compare_adapter.go are godoc comments that
reference the legacy type names while explaining what the new
CompareRuntime/QuorumRuntime types replace. Per the SD-024 Stage 4
runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go), comments
are not flagged — the analyzer fires on (1) forbidden field names on
*Runtime structs, (2) composite literals of legacy types, and (3) function
parameters typed as legacy options. None of those three patterns are
present in any of the three named files.

grade.go and service_run.go contain zero textual matches at all.

The work that satisfied these ACs landed in earlier beads:
  - 35eeebdd  refactor(agent): retire RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith/runFixtureHarnessViaRunner [ddx-b602ccce]
  - 68456595  refactor(agent): switch RunFunc + Run*With APIs to Runtime types [ddx-ce7a796e]
Both are ancestors of base-rev 2b34bfd0.

Verification of the build/test ACs
----------------------------------
- `cd cli && go build ./...` — clean, no output.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1` — the only failure
  is TestIntegration_CodexEcho, which fails on this same base-rev
  (verified with `git checkout 2b34bfd0~1 -- .` and re-running) with
  identical error:
      codex_core::session: failed to record rollout items:
      thread <id> not found
  This is an environment-level failure of the locally-installed `codex`
  binary's session-rollout subsystem, not a regression introduced by
  this bead's notional changes (which are zero). The same test was
  documented as a pre-existing environmental failure in the
  ddx-b602ccce no_changes_rationale committed at dfe1c545 a few hours
  earlier in this same session.

What the description asked for vs what AC verifies
--------------------------------------------------
The expanded description authorizes a full atomic migration of every
ddx-side caller off the three legacy options structs so that B22 can
trivially delete the type definitions in types.go. That broader
migration is NOT what the AC measures; the AC only measures absence of
the three names from three specific files plus build+test.

If the broader migration is the actual intent, it must be tracked by a
bead whose AC names the additional files (agent_runner.go,
agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, virtual.go, script.go,
runner.go, run_internal.go, dispatch.go, models.go, session_index.go,
routing_metrics.go, runtime callers in execute_bead.go and
execute_bead_review.go, plus all *_test.go composite-literal callers).
The current AC's gate (the post-merge AC structural check from
ac_structural_check.go) will green-light this bead as-is.

Recommendation: close as already-satisfied. No code change is required
in this attempt.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Only if the bead author intended the broader migration: re-open or
spawn a successor with AC items that actually name the call sites
(e.g. `field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/runner.go`,
…/agent_runner.go, …/agent_runner_service.go, …/claude_stream.go,
…/virtual.go, …/script.go, …/run_internal.go, …/routing_metrics.go,
…/dispatch.go, …/models.go, …/execute_bead.go, …/execute_bead_review.go,
plus *_test.go composite-literal sites). With those AC items, the
runtimelint analyzer plus a clean build would prove the type
definitions in types.go:36/118/208 are unreachable, enabling B22's
final deletion.

## State of the AC at start of this attempt

The pre-execute-bead checkpoint commit e2f720e8 already contains the
following implementation work (added between the prior attempt's
no_changes return and this attempt's spawn):

        (defaults profile to "cheap" when no routing flags + no project
        config). Verified by TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified
        in cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go (passes, count=1).

  AC2 — Friendly error when global config also empty
        cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: pre-flight at L1625–L1634 returns
        "no providers configured: add at least one provider to
        ~/.config/agent/config.yaml ..." instead of letting fizeau emit
        "routing under-specified". Verified by
        TestZeroConfigWork_NoGlobalProvidersFriendlyError (passes).

  AC3 — Triage gate warning suppressed by default
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: L501–L510 — the
        "warning: triage complexity gate is disabled" line is gone when
        ComplexityGate == nil. Test updated:
        TestComplexityGateNilNoWarning (cli/internal/agent/triage_test.go)
        asserts the legacy warning does not appear (passes).

  AC5 — Coarse-bead handling
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go: Step 0 size-check section
        prepended to both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and
        executeBeadInstructionsAgentText (L1210–L1228 and L1274–L1292).
        Decomposition responsibility now lives in the LLM prompt: the
        agent is instructed to file child beads via `ddx bead create`,
        wire deps via `ddx bead dep add`, annotate the parent, and
        write a no_changes_rationale.txt — exactly the path this very
        attempt is taking.

  AC7 — Integration test
        cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go covers the AC's scenario
        (temp project + .ddx/beads.jsonl, minimal global config,
        `ddx work --local --once`, asserts no "routing under-specified"
        and no .ddx/config.yaml side-effect file).

  AC8 — Documentation update
        website/content/docs/getting-started.md gained a "Drain the
        Queue (Zero-Config)" section presenting the no-flags flow as
        the default, with project-level routing as an advanced
        override.

## What is NOT in checkpoint e2f720e8

  AC4 — Per-bead auto-tier selection from bead metadata
        Currently the auto-route picks one profile ("cheap") for the
        whole loop run. Per-bead tier inference from labels/kind/scope
        is unimplemented. Doing it well requires plumbing per-bead
        routing decisions through the execute-loop singleTierAttempt
        path and adding a small classifier (label-based heuristics +
        catalog tier lookup). This overlaps with the open
        endpoint-first routing redesign (project memory
        project_endpoint_routing_design, 2026-04-21) — ad-hoc tier
        inference now will be redone or block that redesign.

  AC6 — Explicit retry-vs-escalate policy verification
        Existing tier escalation in execute_bead_loop.go does run
        (winning_tier=exhausted events confirm it once dispatched),
        but the AC asks for an explicit policy doc + test that
        transient failures retry within tier and substantive failures
        escalate. No new test was added to lock that contract in.

## Why this attempt makes no new commits

Two reasons:

1. The work that satisfies AC1, AC2, AC3, AC5, AC7, AC8 is already
   on the project branch via checkpoint commit e2f720e8. Re-applying
   it as my own commit is impossible (already committed) and adding a
   cosmetic completion-marker commit just for bead bookkeeping is
   noise the reviewer cannot meaningfully approve.

2. The remaining gaps (AC4, AC6) are each feature-sized and are
   best filed as child beads per the Step 0 instructions the prior
   pass installed. Per memory feedback_no_local_replace and project
   project_endpoint_routing_design, AC4 in particular should not
   land as a bolt-on ahead of the endpoint-first redesign.

## Recommended next action

Close ddx-b790449b as substantially-complete and file two child
beads:

  - ddx-XXXX  Per-bead auto-tier selection from bead metadata
              (depends on / aligned with endpoint-first routing
              redesign). Adds label/kind heuristics + catalog tier
              lookup wired into singleTierAttempt.

  - ddx-XXXX  Lock retry-vs-escalate contract: a regression test
              asserting transient (network/rate-limit/timeout)
              failures retry within tier while substantive
              (probe_ok+exec_fail) failures escalate.

Verification commands run before stopping:

  go build ./...                                                 # green
  go test -run TestZeroConfigWork -count=1 ./cmd                 # PASS
  go test -run TestComplexityGate  -count=1 ./internal/agent     # PASS

Attempt: 20260502T113639-9008e965

Outcome: no_changes (already decomposed)

What is done
============
The umbrella epic ddx-b18d9ce6 has already been decomposed into 18 story-level
child epics (Stories 1-18 from the 2026-05 redesign batch). Every Story epic
already lists ddx-b18d9ce6 as its parent. The acceptance criteria for the
umbrella ("All N sub-epics closed") cannot be satisfied by code in this
attempt — it is satisfied only when the child epics close.

The 18 story-level children, all parent=ddx-b18d9ce6:

  ddx-db5e0227  Story 1:  doc graph visible edges
  ddx-bc346d44  Story 2:  doc graph dark mode visibility
  ddx-86ccbb75  Story 3:  doc graph force-directed layout (no overlap)
  ddx-781af038  Story 4:  doc viewer scope to docs/ + dedupe scanner
  ddx-ffb678fc  Story 5:  artifact viewer categorization by metadata
  ddx-4728ae0f  Story 6:  artifact listing search/sort/filter
  ddx-97335425  Story 7:  artifact listing scale to thousands
  ddx-723d93f2  Story 8:  workers/runs tabs reorganization
  ddx-12e97cb9  Story 9:  agent availability tab
  ddx-e34994e2  Story 10: automatic escalation + always-review
  ddx-64b9a198  Story 11: aggregations on availability page
  ddx-a61bf8ee  Story 12: optimize ddx run/try/work prompts
  ddx-921616ea  Story 13: metric artifact type
  ddx-a038a090  Story 14: server coordinator + clients (multi-node)
  ddx-1d85c927  Story 15: server-side prompt input
  ddx-a8a18cb7  Story 16: expose server task session logs for review
  ddx-43d67aa5  Story 17: artifact-type template/example/prompt panel
  ddx-42b917fe  Story 18: interactive artifact review (template + chat)

(Note: the umbrella description says "17-story batch"; the queue contains 18
story epics tagged phase:2 — Story 18 was filed alongside the original 17.
The acceptance criterion "All 17 sub-epics closed" is read here as "all
phase:2 story epics under this parent close.")

Each story epic in turn has its own filed sub-beads (search labels
`phase:2,story:N`) covering area:web/server/specs/tests/etc. Those are the
work units the drain consumes; the umbrella has no implementation surface
of its own.

What is blocking
================
Nothing structural. The umbrella is an aggregator. It will close once the
18 child story epics close, which depends on the drain working through their
sub-beads.

What a follow-up attempt would need
===================================
Do not re-attempt the umbrella as an implementation bead. Two valid future
moves:

  1. Wait for the drain to close all 18 child story epics, then close
     ddx-b18d9ce6 with `ddx bead close ddx-b18d9ce6` (manual or via a
     "close-when-children-closed" sweep).

Outcome: no_changes (already decomposed)

What is done
============
The umbrella epic ddx-b18d9ce6 has already been decomposed into 18 story-level
child epics (Stories 1-18 from the 2026-05 redesign batch). Every Story epic
already lists ddx-b18d9ce6 as its parent. The acceptance criteria for the
umbrella ("All N sub-epics closed") cannot be satisfied by code in this
attempt — it is satisfied only when the child epics close.

The 18 story-level children, all parent=ddx-b18d9ce6:

  ddx-bc346d44  Story 2:  doc graph dark mode visibility

  ddx-42b917fe  Story 18: interactive artifact review (template + chat)

story epics tagged phase:2 — Story 18 was filed alongside the original 17.

phase:2 story epics under this parent close.")

`phase:2,story:N`) covering area:web/server/specs/tests/etc. Those are the
work units the drain consumes; the umbrella has no implementation surface

================

.ddx/executions/20260504T224346-f69c285d/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260504T224346-f69c285d/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260504T224346-f69c285d/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260504T224346-f69c285d/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260504T224346-f69c285d/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260504T224346-f69c285d/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

reason: Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.
- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above.
- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.
- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above.
- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: |
  ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria
  are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt:

    - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)"
    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed,
  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):
    - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill)
    - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5

  The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are
  children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and
  the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing
  those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be
  done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap —
  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap
  sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that
  warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed.

  Recommended next action for the human triager:
    1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md
       (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section.
    2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead
       (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`.
    3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable
       once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied.

  No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week
  soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this
  worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.

  Wired-in integration test TestWorkersPanel_LiveTransitions —
  starts a real worker, asserts connected→stale (after 2× probe
  interval)→disconnected (after 10× probe interval) using a

  fixture-based. Depends on 5a. ~150 LOC + small clock seam.

testable in isolation and unlocks the other two. 5b and 5c each

  Artifact: website/layouts/index.html
  Sections present (grep '<!-- Section':

    3. .ddx-home-how         (How it works — 3 steps)

    5. .ddx-home-principles  (6-card principles grid)
    6. .ddx-home-demo        (Asciinema player)
    7. .ddx-home-cta         (Get started CTA)
  Cross-check: website/DESIGN.md "Homepage Section Map" table lists the same 7
  selectors. Playwright test 'homepage has all 7 sections' asserts each.

AC 2 — /features/ section with maturity badges
  Artifact: website/content/features/_index.md
  Seven feature sections, each ending in a {{< maturity ... >}} shortcode call:
    Artifact Graph (beta), Beads & DAG (stable), Execute-Loop (stable),
    Evidence Capture (stable), Multi-Model Review (beta), Skills (beta),
    Agent-Agnostic Dispatch (stable).
  Shortcode: website/layouts/shortcodes/maturity.html
  Playwright test 'features page loads with maturity badges' asserts visibility
  of .ddx-maturity--stable, .ddx-maturity--beta, .ddx-maturity--planned.

AC 3 — DESIGN.md tokens wired to Hugo
  Artifact: website/DESIGN.md
  Sections: Palette (lever/load/fulcrum/brass), Surface & Foreground,
  Typography (Inter/Space Grotesk/Newsreader), Homepage Section Map,
  Maturity Badges, Responsive Breakpoints.
  Wiring: layouts/index.html:1 imports assets/css/custom.css through
    {{- $css := resources.Get "css/custom.css" | minify | fingerprint -}}
  Tokens (--ddx-accent-lever, --ddx-bg-canvas, etc.) are defined in
  assets/css/custom.css and consumed by .ddx-home-* selectors in layouts/.

AC 4 — Playwright e2e gaps closed
  Artifact: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts (134 lines, 12 tests).
  Coverage:
    - homepage loads with hero and features
    - homepage has all 7 sections (per-section locator assertions)
    - homepage screenshot (full-page, fonts/CDN blocked for stability)
    - homepage fits mobile viewport (390x844, no horizontal scroll)
    - features nav link in homepage
    - features page loads with maturity badges (stable/beta/planned)
    - features page screenshot
    - getting started page
    - CLI reference page
    - skills page
    - plugins page
    - ecosystem page
    - nav links work (Docs / Concepts / Features routing)
  Snapshots committed: e2e/microsite.spec.ts-snapshots/{features,getting-started,homepage}-linux.png

AC 5 — UI screenshots committed to website/static/ui/
  Artifacts:
    website/static/ui/homepage.png
    website/static/ui/features.png
  These are the canonical promotional screenshots referenced from marketing
  copy. Playwright snapshot baselines (e2e/microsite.spec.ts-snapshots/) are
  the test-time visual regression baselines.

  Residual gap (not blocking AC, noted for follow-up): content/features/_index.md
  references seven additional feature-specific placeholder images
  (feature-artifact-graph.png, feature-beads-dag.png, feature-execute-loop.png,
  feature-evidence-capture.png, feature-multi-model-review.png,
  feature-skills.png, feature-agent-dispatch.png) that do not yet exist in
  static/ui/. The alt text on each marks them "placeholder". Recommend a
  follow-up bead to either (a) generate per-feature screenshots, or (b) drop
  the placeholder image lines until assets are produced.

What is done
  All five acceptance criteria are evidenced by files currently in the
  worktree as listed above. Repository state at base aff3240b is sufficient
  to close this epic.

What is blocking a per-attempt commit
  Nothing in the AC requires new code in this attempt. Producing a fabricated
  diff to "show progress" would be lower quality than a clean no_changes
  exit with this rationale.

What a follow-up attempt would need
  Either (a) close this epic on the strength of in-tree evidence (recommended),
  or (b) split the residual feature-image gap into a fresh bead targeting only
  static/ui/feature-*.png generation and drop this rollup's hold on the queue.

.ddx/executions/20260504T215650-71fd1d81/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement. In the coordinator topology (Story 14), the originating peer identity attestation is not forwarded from coordinator to client — any non-localhost write goes through the hub without carrying who initiated it. The spoke cannot authorize on both origin and coordinator identity because neither is transmitted.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: current federation supports query fan-out only. No RouteMutationToProjectOwner, no ForwardMutation, no identity header propagation.
- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.
- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.

PLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):
- Multi-node routing (point 6): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations.
- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.
- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.

This bead has been decomposed into 4 children:
- ddx-468d800f — S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers.
- ddx-eb75a32d — S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation).
- ddx-ba31fec8 — S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization.
- ddx-ea1ada47 — S15-7d: Integration test with two in-process servers.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT spec; changes go into FEAT-010 and FEAT-011 extensions.
- Performance optimization of federation routing.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior (already shipped in fanout.go).",

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

1	package bead
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"bufio"
     5		"bytes"
     6		"encoding/json"
     7		"fmt"
     8		"os"
     9		"path/filepath"
    10		"sort"
    11		"strings"
    12		"time"
    13	)
    14	
    15	// BackendAxon selects the axon-backed RawBackend. Per TD-030 D3 the on-disk
    16	// shape splits beads from their events: one row per bead in the
    17	// ddx_beads collection, plus one entity per event in the
    18	// ddx_bead_events collection linked back to its bead via event_of.
    19	//
    20	// The implementation in axon_backend.go is an in-process emulation of the
    21	// axon GraphQL surface. It serializes the two collections to JSONL files
    22	// under <dir>/axon/ and acquires a single directory lock around mutations.
    23	// The wire-protocol path (genqlient against a real axon server, per TD-030
    24	// §"Wire transport") is the GA delivery; this RawBackend keeps the same
    25	// two-collection split and OCC-compatible bead row schema so the wire path
    26	// is a contained refactor.
    27	//
    28	// AC §3 ("Behind a feature flag until ddx-3ec0chaos signs off"): NewStore
    29	// only routes to AxonBackend when the operator opts in via
    30	// DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL=1. With the flag unset, beads.backend=axon falls
    31	// through to the JSONL default and a stderr warning is emitted so the
    32	// misconfiguration is visible without breaking the workspace.
    33	const BackendAxon = "axon"
    34	
    35	// AxonExperimentalEnv gates whether the axon backend may be selected. The
    36	// flag is intentionally separate from beads.backend so the chaos-test bead
    37	// (ddx-743bc194) can flip operators to axon machine-by-machine without
    38	// editing every project's config.
    39	const AxonExperimentalEnv = "DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL"
    40	
    41	// axonSchemaVersion is written into every persisted entity. Schema upgrades
    42	// are handled lazy-on-read until axon FEAT-017 ships native schema
    43	// migration (per TD-030 v1 policy on schema versioning).
    44	const axonSchemaVersion = 1
    45	
    46	// AxonDirName is the per-store axon subdirectory under .ddx that holds the
    47	// two-collection snapshot files and the lock dir.
    48	const AxonDirName = "axon"
    49	
    50	// AxonBeadsCollection / AxonEventsCollection are the logical collection names
    51	// from TD-030's schema mapping. They are surfaced as the on-disk filenames so
    52	// inspecting the directory tells the operator which collection an entity
    53	// belongs to.
    54	const (
    55		AxonBeadsCollection  = "ddx_beads"
    56		AxonEventsCollection = "ddx_bead_events"
    57	)
    58	
    59	// AxonBackend stores the bead corpus in axon's two-collection shape.
    60	//
    61	// On WriteAll the backend splits each bead's inline Extra["events"] into one
    62	// entity per event under ddx_bead_events and persists the bead row (sans
    63	// inline events) under ddx_beads. ReadAll re-merges events back into
    64	// Extra["events"] so the higher-level Store and the chaos suite see the
    65	// uniform inline-events shape they expect.
    66	//
    67	// Beads with Extra[events_attachment] set (post-Close externalisation) are
    68	// persisted with their events array empty in axon's events collection. The
    69	// attachment file remains the canonical source for those events so Store's
    70	// existing read path (eventsForBead) keeps working unchanged.
    71	type AxonBackend struct {
    72		Dir        string
    73		BeadsFile  string
    74		EventsFile string
    75		LockDir    string
    76		LockWait   time.Duration
    77	}
    78	
    79	// Compile-time check: AxonBackend satisfies RawBackend.
    80	var _ RawBackend = (*AxonBackend)(nil)
    81	
    82	// NewAxonBackend constructs an axon-backed RawBackend rooted at dir. dir is
    83	// the .ddx directory for the project; collection files and the lock live
    84	// under <dir>/axon/.
    85	func NewAxonBackend(dir string, lockWait time.Duration) *AxonBackend {
    86		root := filepath.Join(dir, AxonDirName)
    87		return &AxonBackend{
    88			Dir:        root,
    89			BeadsFile:  filepath.Join(root, AxonBeadsCollection+".jsonl"),
    90			EventsFile: filepath.Join(root, AxonEventsCollection+".jsonl"),
    91			LockDir:    filepath.Join(root, ".lock"),
    92			LockWait:   lockWait,
    93		}
    94	}
    95	
    96	// AxonExperimentalEnabled reports whether the operator has opted in to the
    97	// experimental axon backend via DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL=1. NewStore consults
    98	// this before routing beads.backend=axon to AxonBackend.
    99	func AxonExperimentalEnabled() bool {
   100		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(AxonExperimentalEnv))) {
   101		case "1", "true", "yes", "on":
   102			return true
   103		}
   104		return false
   105	}
   106	
   107	func (a *AxonBackend) Init() error {
   108		if err := os.MkdirAll(a.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
   109			return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon init dir: %w", err)
   110		}
   111		for _, path := range []string{a.BeadsFile, a.EventsFile} {
   112			f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
   113			if err != nil {
   114				return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon init %s: %w", path, err)
   115			}
   116			if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
   117				return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon close %s: %w", path, err)
   118			}
   119		}
   120		return nil
   121	}
   122	
   123	// ReadAll loads bead rows and event entities from disk and re-merges events
   124	// back into each bead's Extra["events"]. Orphan events (event_of pointing at
   125	// a missing bead, e.g. after a partial-write crash) are silently dropped.
   126	func (a *AxonBackend) ReadAll() ([]Bead, error) {
   127		beads, err := a.readBeadRows()
   128		if err != nil {
   129			return nil, err
   130		}
   131		events, err := a.readEventEntities()
   132		if err != nil {
   133			return nil, err
   134		}
   135		// Index beads by id so we can attach events and drop orphans.
   136		byID := make(map[string]*Bead, len(beads))
   137		for i := range beads {
   138			byID[beads[i].ID] = &beads[i]
   139		}
   140		// Group events by bead id, preserving insertion order on disk.
   141		grouped := make(map[string][]BeadEvent, len(events))
   142		order := make(map[string]int, len(events))
   143		for _, ev := range events {
   144			if _, ok := byID[ev.beadID]; !ok {
   145				continue // orphan
   146			}
   147			grouped[ev.beadID] = append(grouped[ev.beadID], ev.event)
   148			if _, seen := order[ev.beadID]; !seen {
   149				order[ev.beadID] = len(order)
   150			}
   151		}
   152		for id, evs := range grouped {
   153			b := byID[id]
   154			if b.Extra == nil {
   155				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
   156			}
   157			// Skip beads whose events have been externalised — the attachment file
   158			// is the canonical source post-close and inlining stale events would
   159			// duplicate the history.
   160			if hasEventsAttachment(b) {
   161				continue
   162			}
   163			b.Extra["events"] = encodeEventsForExtra(evs)
   164		}
   165		return beads, nil
   166	}
   167	
   168	// WriteAll splits inline events from each bead and persists both collections.
   169	// Writes are serialised at the directory-lock layer (WithLock); this method
   170	// rewrites both files in temp+rename style so a crash mid-write leaves the
   171	// previous snapshot intact rather than producing torn rows.
   172	func (a *AxonBackend) WriteAll(beads []Bead) error {
   173		if err := os.MkdirAll(a.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
   174			return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon mkdir: %w", err)
   175		}
   176		sort.Slice(beads, func(i, j int) bool { return beads[i].ID < beads[j].ID })
   177	
   178		var beadsBuf, eventsBuf bytes.Buffer
   179		for _, b := range beads {
   180			// Extract and strip inline events for the split.
   181			var events []BeadEvent
   182			if b.Extra != nil {
   183				if raw, ok := b.Extra["events"]; ok {
   184					events = decodeBeadEvents(raw)
   185				}
   186			}
   187			stripped := beadWithoutInlineEvents(b)
   188			row, err := axonEncodeBead(stripped)
   189			if err != nil {
   190				return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon marshal %s: %w", b.ID, err)
   191			}
   192			beadsBuf.Write(row)
   193			beadsBuf.WriteByte('\n')
   194	
   195			for i, ev := range events {
   196				data, err := axonEncodeEvent(b.ID, i, ev)
   197				if err != nil {
   198					return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon marshal event %s/%d: %w", b.ID, i, err)
   199				}
   200				eventsBuf.Write(data)
   201				eventsBuf.WriteByte('\n')
   202			}
   203		}
   204	
   205		// Write events first so a crash between the two writes leaves orphan
   206		// events (which ReadAll silently drops) rather than a bead row whose
   207		// events have not landed yet — favouring the safer side of the partial-
   208		// write window for an audit log.
   209		if err := writeAtomicFile(a.EventsFile, eventsBuf.Bytes()); err != nil {
   210			return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon write events: %w", err)
   211		}
   212		if err := writeAtomicFile(a.BeadsFile, beadsBuf.Bytes()); err != nil {
   213			return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon write beads: %w", err)
   214		}
   215		return nil
   216	}

/bin/zsh -lc "set -euo pipefail
labels='phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:feature, blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d, blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2'
parent='ddx-8d747049'

create_bead() {
  title=\""'$1"
  description="$2"
  acceptance="$3"
  ddx bead create "$title" --type task --parent "$parent" --labels "$labels" --description "$description" --acceptance "$acceptance"
}

id1=$(create_bead '"\\
  'bead: scaffold cli/internal/bead/axon genqlient client and subscriptions' \\
  \"PROBLEM\\nThe Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.\\n\\nROOT CAUSE\\ncli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.\\n\\nPROPOSED FIX\\n- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.\\n- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.\\n- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.\\n- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.\\n\\nNON-SCOPE\\n- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.\\n- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.\\n- Axon server-side schema work.\"
  \"1. cli/internal/bead/axon/ exists with schema.graphql, query documents, genqlient config, and generated Go bindings.\\n2. TestAxonClient_SchemaBindingsCompile and TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsStream are added in cli/internal/bead/axon/.\\n3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/axon/... passes.\\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\")

echo \""'$id1"

id2=$(create_bead '"\\
  'bead: implement cli/internal/bead Axon backend operations on the generated GraphQL client' \\
  \"PROBLEM\\nThe current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.\\n\\nROOT CAUSE\\ncli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.\\n\\nPROPOSED FIX\\n- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.\\n- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.\\n- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.\\n- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.\\n\\nNON-SCOPE\\n- Backend selection config plumbing.\\n- Package test matrix broadening.\\n- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.\"
  \"1. TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet, TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists, TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim, TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree, TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection, and TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip pass against the real Axon-backed implementation.\\n2. backend_axon.go no longer uses local JSONL snapshots as the primary storage path.\\n3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.\\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\")

echo \""'$id2"

id3=$(create_bead '"\\
  'bead: wire cli/internal/bead backend selection and .ddx config for Axon' \\

The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.\\n\\nPROPOSED FIX\\n- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.\\n- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.\\n- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.\\n- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.\\n\\nNON-SCOPE\\n- GraphQL client generation.\\n- Axon backend CRUD internals.\\n- Subscription transport.\"
  \"1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.\\n2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.\\n3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.\\n4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.\\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\")

echo \""'$id3"

id4=$(create_bead '"\\
  'bead: parameterize cli/internal/bead conformance suites across jsonl and axon' \\
  \"PROBLEM\\nThe package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.\\n\\nROOT CAUSE\\ncli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.\\n\\nPROPOSED FIX\\n- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.\\n- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.\\n- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.\\n\\nNON-SCOPE\\n- GraphQL client generation.\\n- Backend selection/config plumbing.\\n- Subscription integration smoke tests.\"
  \"1. TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost run under both jsonl and axon subtests.\\n2. The package conformance helpers make the backend selector explicit instead of silently defaulting in only one path.\\n3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.\\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\")

echo \""'$id4"

id5=$(create_bead '"\\
  'bead: add cli/internal/bead/axon WebSocket subscription integration smoke test' \\
  \"PROBLEM\\nTD-030 requires a live GraphQL subscription path so DDx can subscribe to Axon changeEvents for bead and bead-event updates. The current test suite in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go covers CRUD, deps, events, and import/export, but there is no subscription smoke test yet.\\n\\nROOT CAUSE\\ncli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go:131-179 verifies the split event collection, but nothing in the package opens a WebSocket subscription and asserts that changeEvents are delivered in order. That leaves the live-update path unexercised.\\n\\nPROPOSED FIX\\n- Add an integration test in cli/internal/bead/axon/ that opens the subscription endpoint, performs a small bead mutation sequence, and asserts the expected changeEvents arrive.\\n- Reuse the generated Axon client and subscription transport from the client-scaffolding bead.\\n- Keep the test isolated and deterministic so it can run in the normal package suite.\\n\\nNON-SCOPE\\n- Core backend CRUD logic.\\n- Config selection.\\n- Package-wide backend matrix work.\"
  \"1. TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsStream or TestAxonSubscription_ReceivesBeadMutations is added and passes.\\n2. The subscription smoke test uses the generated Axon client and a WebSocket transport.\\n3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/axon/... passes.\\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\")

echo \""'$id5"
'

EOF

printf '"'%s
